items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
# ZYNQ.peer

Lightweight, secure and stable WebRTC P2P library built on PeerJS. Provides simple 1-to-1 messaging and optional audio/video streaming with zero backend.

### Key Improvements
- Factory + simple constructor compatibility (no API breakage)
- Built-in abuse protection: rate limiting, max message size (64KB), non-string payload rejection
- Connection state machine (DISCONNECTED → CONNECTING → CONNECTED → RECONNECTING → FAILED)
- Exponential backoff + jitter auto-reconnect
- Single shared `getUserMedia` stream (no duplicate calls)
- Microtask / optional RAF data event batching
- Full listener cleanup and memory-leak protection
- Handshake-ready flag and safe `send()`
- Debug mode with detailed lifecycle logging

items.label: Quick Start
Core API
items.content:
# Core API

### Constructor (simple mode)
```javascript
new ZYNQ.peer(config)
```
Config options:
```javascript
{
  video: boolean,
  audio: boolean,
  txt: boolean,
  autoReconnect: boolean,
  maxReconnectAttempts: number,
  debug: boolean,
  rateLimit: number,      // messages per second
  maxMsgSize: number,     // bytes (default 65536)
  rafBatching: boolean    // use requestAnimationFrame for data events
}
```

### Factory (advanced mode)
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.get(remoteId, config)
```
Returns the same instance for the same `remoteId`.

### Connect
```javascript
engine.connect(remoteId?)  // remoteId only required in simple mode
```

### Send
```javascript
engine.send(message)  // string only, safe when closed
```

### Close / Destroy
```javascript
engine.close()     // stops reconnect, clears state
engine.destroy()   // full cleanup + removes from global registry
```

items.label: Events
items.content:
# Events

### Listen
```javascript
engine.on('event-name', callback)
```

### Available Events

| Event | Data | Description |
|----------------|-----------------------------------|-----------|
| `ready` | `id` | Local PeerJS ID assigned |
| `open` | `remoteId` | Data connection opened |
| `data` | `message` | Received data (batched) |
| `stream` | `MediaStream` | Incoming remote media stream |
| `localStream` | `MediaStream` | Local camera/mic stream created |
| `close` | `remoteId` | Connection closed |
| `error` | `{type, code, error?}` | Any error (data/media/reconnect) |
| `reconnecting` | `{attempt, delay}` | Auto-reconnect started |
| `sent` | `message` | Message successfully sent |

**Notes**
- `data` events are automatically batched (microtask or RAF)
- All events are safe after `destroy()`
- `error` objects contain structured `type` and `code`

items.label: Messaging
Audio & Video
Connections
items.content:
# Connection Management

### State machine
```javascript
console.log(engine.state); // DISCONNECTED | CONNECTING | CONNECTED | RECONNECTING | FAILED
engine.active // boolean (data or media connected)
```

### Disconnect
```javascript
engine.close(); // stops auto-reconnect
```

### Auto-reconnect
- Exponential backoff with jitter (max 5 attempts by default)
- Stops automatically if `close()` was called manually
- Emits `reconnecting` and `error` events

items.label: System & Advanced
items.content:
# System & Advanced

### Debug mode
```javascript
const engine = new ZYNQ.peer({ ..., debug: true });
```
Logs connection lifecycle, reconnect attempts, stream events and security violations.

### Global cleanup
```javascript
engine.destroy();
ZYNQ.peer.destroyAll(); // destroys every engine + PeerJS instance
```

### Performance & Safety
- Microtask batching for `data` events (or RAF when `rafBatching: true`)
- All listeners removed on close/destroy
- Race-condition safe async operations
- `send()` never throws when connection is closed

**Notes**
- No backend or signaling server required
- Fully compatible with existing simple-app usage
- All improvements are internal – public API unchanged
